title: Backup verified, crypto quiet, cron reliability still needs cleanup
summary: April 10 was mostly an automation and continuity day, not a build-output day. No new quote captures or reports landed in the external Ava workspace, but the daily memory backup completed cleanly and verified the 2026-04-10 archive in the HI Ava Drive folder. The crypto signal cron ran and returned NO_SIGNAL, while Weekly Memory Distill remained the main unresolved automation issue because of its invalid model reference.
keyDecisions: Treat April 10 as an operations and monitoring day rather than a production-output day.
Count the verified daily memory backup as the primary completed action for the day.
Keep separating healthy routine automations from unresolved cron reliability issues instead of treating all green runs as full system health.
openItems: Repair the Weekly Memory Distill model reference before its next scheduled Saturday run.
Keep watching outbound Discord reliability for cron deliveries.
Confirm the Nightly Session Journal continues to complete cleanly after tonight.

title: Quiet ops day, backup clean, journal auth broke

title: Backup Policy Block + Discord Delivery Still Broken

No new FF&E job files, quote captures, or report outputs landed in the Ava workspace, so the signal came from cron review instead. The daily backup created its archive successfully but stopped short of upload because the current Google Workspace rules mark that account as read-only, while the evening crypto alert returned NO_SIGNAL and still could not deliver to Discord.
decisions: Treat April 6 as an infrastructure/continuity day, not a production-work day
Honor the read-only Google Workspace rule in TOOLS.md even when a cron prompt requests Drive upload
Keep Discord outbound repair on the active open-items list until cron delivery actually works
No new sourcing, procurement, or project-direction decisions were recorded from local Ava workspace files today

Repair Discord outbound/channel support for cron delivery

title: Config Patch + Cron Triage + Memory Checkpoints
summary: April 4 was mostly an infrastructure and continuity day. OpenClaw was patched to keep openai-codex/gpt-5.4 as the primary model while adding a Claude fallback, and memory/checkpoint files were updated throughout the day. Cron review showed the weekly distill failing on a bad model reference, the daily backup still timing out, and Discord/Telegram delivery paths still needing cleanup.
decisions: Keep OpenAI (openai-codex/gpt-5.4) as the primary model for now
Add Claude only as a fallback until auth/model routing is stable
Treat Telegram restoration as a credential issue because the live token is missing/redacted
Prioritize cron reliability before trusting automated delivery
